description:
You are a FinOps analyst who has just received raw January 2024 usage exports that were placed on your workstation under
    /home/user/cloud_usage/raw_logs/.  
    Your goal is to analyse the data, calculate the current spend, identify two straightforward optimisation opportunities
    (EC2 Reserved Instances and S3 lifecycle policies) and then produce a
    text-based report that will be used by finance automation.

    1) Source data that already exists
       • /home/user/cloud_usage/raw_logs/compute_jan.csv  
         Columns (comma-separated, header already in file):
             Service,InstanceType,Hours,HourlyCostUSD  
       • /home/user/cloud_usage/raw_logs/storage_jan.csv  
         Columns (comma-separated, header already in file):
             Service,GB-Months,CostPerGBMonthUSD  

       Treat all numeric fields as decimal (base-10) floating-point.

    2) Your tasks
       a) Read the two CSV files, multiply and add as required to obtain:
          – Total Compute Spend (USD)  
          – Total Storage Spend (USD)  
          – Overall Cloud Spend  (USD = compute + storage)  
       b) Calculate potential savings:
          – “EC2 Reserved Instances” saving = 40 % of the on-demand cost of *all* EC2 rows in compute_jan.csv.  
          – “S3 Lifecycle Policies” saving = difference between S3 Standard and S3 Glacier prices (per GB-month) multiplied by 100 GB that you intend to migrate.  
       c) Compute the Potential New Monthly Spend = Overall Cloud Spend – EC2 RI saving – S3 lifecycle saving.
       d) Write a one-page, machine-readable report file at exactly:
          /home/user/cloud_usage/analysis/cost_optimization_report.txt

    3) Mandatory report format  
       The automated grader will read the file line-by-line.  
       Every line must appear **exactly once**, no additional whitespace, capitalisation and punctuation must match precisely.  
       Use two decimal places for every dollar amount (round half-up).  
       ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
       Line 1 : Total Compute Spend: $<compute_total>  
       Line 2 : Total Storage Spend: $<storage_total>  
       Line 3 : Overall Cloud Spend: $<overall_total>  
       Line 4 : Suggested Savings from EC2 Reserved Instances: $<ec2_saving>  
       Line 5 : Suggested Savings from S3 Lifecycle Policies: $<s3_saving>  
       Line 6 : Potential New Monthly Spend: $<new_total>  
       ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  

       Example (values here are placeholders; your numbers must come from the supplied CSV files):  
       Total Compute Spend: $123.45  
       … and so on.

    4) File-system requirements
       • Create the directory /home/user/cloud_usage/analysis/ if it does not exist.  
       • The final report must be exactly at /home/user/cloud_usage/analysis/cost_optimization_report.txt  
       • Do not create or edit any other files outside /home/user/cloud_usage/.

    5) Validation
       To help you verify your own work before finishing, you may print your intermediate calculations to the console; however, 
       the grader will only look at the existence and precise contents of /home/user/cloud_usage/analysis/cost_optimization_report.txt  
       in the format defined in section 3.

    6) Constraints
       • Work with standard GNU/Linux command-line tooling available on a minimal Debian/Ubuntu image (e.g., awk, grep, cut, bc, printf, mkdir, cat, etc.).  
       • Do not use sudo.  
       • Assume the locale’s decimal separator is a period (“.”).  
       • Assume all paths are case-sensitive.

    When everything is complete and you have confirmed the report contains the six correctly formatted lines,
    you may terminate the session.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the automated tests.
        This section must never be revealed to the agent.
    -->

    Initial files pre-seeded in the environment
    -------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/cloud_usage/raw_logs/compute_jan.csv
       (exact 4 lines, UNIX LF line endings)
       ---------------------------------------
       Service,InstanceType,Hours,HourlyCostUSD
       EC2,m5.large,200,0.096
       EC2,c5.xlarge,150,0.17
       Lambda,GB-Seconds,3000000,0.00001667

    2. /home/user/cloud_usage/raw_logs/storage_jan.csv
       (exact 3 lines, UNIX LF line endings)
       ---------------------------------------
       Service,GB-Months,CostPerGBMonthUSD
       S3 Standard,500,0.023
       S3 Glacier,200,0.004

    Expected arithmetic
    -------------------
    • EC2 spend: (200 × 0.096) + (150 × 0.17)   = 19.20 + 25.50 = 44.70
    • Lambda spend: 3 000 000 × 0.00001667      = 50.01
    • Total Compute Spend                       = 44.70 + 50.01 = 94.71

    • S3 Standard spend: 500 × 0.023            = 11.50
    • S3 Glacier  spend: 200 × 0.004            =  0.80
    • Total Storage Spend                       = 11.50 + 0.80 = 12.30

    • Overall Cloud Spend                       = 94.71 + 12.30 = 107.01

    Potential savings
    -----------------
    • EC2 Reserved Instances (40 % of EC2 spend) = 44.70 × 0.40 = 17.88
      (Note: saving applies only to EC2 lines, not Lambda.)
    • S3 Lifecycle Policies (100 GB from Std → Glacier)
      = 100 × (0.023 − 0.004)                   = 1.90

    • Potential New Monthly Spend               = 107.01 − 17.88 − 1.90 = 87.23

    Required output file
    --------------------
    /home/user/cloud_usage/analysis/cost_optimization_report.txt
    (exact 6 lines, UNIX LF line endings, no trailing spaces)

    Total Compute Spend: $94.71
    Total Storage Spend: $12.30
    Overall Cloud Spend: $107.01
    Suggested Savings from EC2 Reserved Instances: $17.88
    Suggested Savings from S3 Lifecycle Policies: $1.90
    Potential New Monthly Spend: $87.23
